DIAGRAMTABLE_INCLUDECUSTOMTYPESOFROWTYPES_PROPERTY
[@Deprecated](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html)public static final [String](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/String.html) DIAGRAMTABLE_INCLUDECUSTOMTYPESOFROWTYPES_PROPERTY
Deprecated.
use DiagramTableStereotype.INCLUDECUSTOMTYPESOFROWTYPES
Select to include the custom types that extend the specified row element types. For example, if you specify a Package as a row element type, the custom types, such as a Smart Package, extending the Package will also be included.
See Also:
[Constant Field Values](../../../constant-values.html#com.nomagic.uml2.MagicDrawProfile.DIAGRAMTABLE_INCLUDECUSTOMTYPESOFROWTYPES_PROPERTY)

Deprecated.
use DiagramTableStereotype.INCLUDESUBTYPESOFROWTYPES
Select to include the subtypes of the specified row element types. For example, if you specify a Package as a row element type, its subtypes, such as a Model and Profile, will also be included.
See Also:
[Constant Field Values](../../../constant-values.html#com.nomagic.uml2.MagicDrawProfile.DIAGRAMTABLE_INCLUDESUBTYPESOFROWTYPES_PROPERTY)

use NumberingSchemeStereotype.NUMBERINGSTYLE
Select a multi-lever style to number elements in more than one numbering level. Specify at least one number part for each numbering level.
 Select a consecutive style to number elements in one numbering level. Specify only one number part.

Deprecated.
use RelationMapStereotype.INCLUDECUSTOMTYPES
Select to include the custom types that extend the specified element types. For example, if you specify a Package as an element type, the custom types, such as a Smart Package, extending the Package will also be included.
See Also:
[Constant Field Values](../../../constant-values.html#com.nomagic.uml2.MagicDrawProfile.RELATIONMAP_INCLUDECUSTOMTYPES_PROPERTY)

Deprecated.
use RelationMapStereotype.INCLUDESUBTYPES
Select to include the subtypes of the specified element types. For example, if you specify a Package as an element type, its subtypes, such as a Model and Profile, will also be included.
See Also:
[Constant Field Values](../../../constant-values.html#com.nomagic.uml2.MagicDrawProfile.RELATIONMAP_INCLUDESUBTYPES_PROPERTY)

SMARTPACKAGE_USEINSELECTIONDIALOGS_PROPERTY
[@Deprecated](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html)public static final [String](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/String.html) SMARTPACKAGE_USEINSELECTIONDIALOGS_PROPERTY
Deprecated.
use SmartPackageStereotype.USEINSELECTIONDIALOGS
This option determines whether contents of this smart package should be calculated and taken into account when operating in various Select Element dialogs. Having this option on may help you to pick elements in Select Element dialog, but it also incurs performance penalty. If your smart package contains a slow-running computation - such as Find - you may want to keep this option off for performance reasons.
See Also:
[Constant Field Values](../../../constant-values.html#com.nomagic.uml2.MagicDrawProfile.SMARTPACKAGE_USEINSELECTIONDIALOGS_PROPERTY)

DERIVEDPROPERTYSPECIFICATION_STEREOTYPE

See Also:

DERIVEDPROPERTYSPECIFICATION_EXPRESSION_PROPERTY
[@Deprecated](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html)public static final [String](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/String.html) DERIVEDPROPERTYSPECIFICATION_EXPRESSION_PROPERTY
Deprecated.
use DerivedPropertySpecificationStereotype.EXPRESSION
List of different types of criteria how to gather dependent elements: Simple (UML Relation, Property or Tag); Meta Chain, OCL and Binary. If several criterion are defined results is union of all of them.
 Expression can be used in Derived Property Specification, Dependency Matrix, Dependency Map.
See Also:
[Constant Field Values](../../../constant-values.html#com.nomagic.uml2.MagicDrawProfile.DERIVEDPROPERTYSPECIFICATION_EXPRESSION_PROPERTY)
DERIVEDPROPERTYSPECIFICATION_ISREADONLY_PROPERTY
[@Deprecated](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html)public static final [String](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/String.html) DERIVEDPROPERTYSPECIFICATION_ISREADONLY_PROPERTY
Deprecated.
use DerivedPropertySpecificationStereotype.ISREADONLY
If True, property is read only without ability to create target element. If False Property is read / write with ability to create value according to defined expressions.
See Also:
[Constant Field Values](../../../constant-values.html#com.nomagic.uml2.MagicDrawProfile.DERIVEDPROPERTYSPECIFICATION_ISREADONLY_PROPERTY)

PROPERTYGROUP_STEREOTYPE
[@Deprecated](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html)public static final [String](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/String.html) PROPERTYGROUP_STEREOTYPE

PROPERTYGROUP_COLUMNS_PROPERTY
[@Deprecated](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html)public static final [String](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/String.html) PROPERTYGROUP_COLUMNS_PROPERTY
Deprecated.
use PropertyGroupStereotype.COLUMNS
p {padding:0px; margin:0px;}
Stores the specified table columns for showing the values of a multivalued property in the element’s Specification window, for example, **ownedOperation** in Class.
 
**Note:** Do not specify this property, when there is more than one property assigned to the properties group and/ or subgroup, i.e., when there is more than one value added to the **Properties** property.
 
**Note:** The property group, to which the multivalued property is assigned, must be specified as a separate tab in the element’s Specification window, for example, the **Use As Node** property must be set to *true*.
See Also:
[Constant Field Values](../../../constant-values.html#com.nomagic.uml2.MagicDrawProfile.PROPERTYGROUP_COLUMNS_PROPERTY)
PROPERTYGROUP_FILTER_PROPERTY
[@Deprecated](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html)public static final [String](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/String.html) PROPERTYGROUP_FILTER_PROPERTY
Deprecated.
use PropertyGroupStereotype.FILTER
p {padding:0px; margin:0px;}
Stores the element types (UML metaclasses and custom stereotypes) that will be displayed as property values.
See Also:
[Constant Field Values](../../../constant-values.html#com.nomagic.uml2.MagicDrawProfile.PROPERTYGROUP_FILTER_PROPERTY)
PROPERTYGROUP_PROPERTIES_PROPERTY
[@Deprecated](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html)public static final [String](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/String.html) PROPERTYGROUP_PROPERTIES_PROPERTY
Deprecated.
use PropertyGroupStereotype.PROPERTIES
p {padding:0px; margin:0px;}
Stores a list of properties that will be visible in the property group or subgroup.
 
**Note:** Do not set this property value to *true* for the property subgroup.
See Also:
[Constant Field Values](../../../constant-values.html#com.nomagic.uml2.MagicDrawProfile.PROPERTYGROUP_PROPERTIES_PROPERTY)
PROPERTYGROUP_SHOWGROUPINCOMPARTMENTEDIT_PROPERTY
[@Deprecated](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html)public static final [String](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/String.html) PROPERTYGROUP_SHOWGROUPINCOMPARTMENTEDIT_PROPERTY
Deprecated.
use PropertyGroupStereotype.SHOWGROUPINCOMPARTMENTEDIT
p {padding:0px; margin:0px;}
This property allows for displaying the element properties in the **Compartment Edit** dialog > the **Element Properties** tab and in a note on a diagram either within the property group/subgroup or without it. If the value is *true*, the property name will be displayed as follows: *<property_group_name>::<property_name>*. If the value is *false*, the property name will be displayed as follows: *<property_name>*.
See Also:
[Constant Field Values](../../../constant-values.html#com.nomagic.uml2.MagicDrawProfile.PROPERTYGROUP_SHOWGROUPINCOMPARTMENTEDIT_PROPERTY)
PROPERTYGROUP_SHOWGROUPINDEPENDENCYMATRIX_PROPERTY

Deprecated.
use PropertyGroupStereotype.SHOWGROUPINDEPENDENCYMATRIX
See Also:
[Constant Field Values](../../../constant-values.html#com.nomagic.uml2.MagicDrawProfile.PROPERTYGROUP_SHOWGROUPINDEPENDENCYMATRIX_PROPERTY)
PROPERTYGROUP_SHOWGROUPINELEMENTSPECIFICATION_PROPERTY
[@Deprecated](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html)public static final [String](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/String.html) PROPERTYGROUP_SHOWGROUPINELEMENTSPECIFICATION_PROPERTY
Deprecated.
use PropertyGroupStereotype.SHOWGROUPINELEMENTSPECIFICATION
p {padding:0px; margin:0px;}
If the value is *true*, the property group/ subgroup will be visible in the element’s Specification window.
See Also:
[Constant Field Values](../../../constant-values.html#com.nomagic.uml2.MagicDrawProfile.PROPERTYGROUP_SHOWGROUPINELEMENTSPECIFICATION_PROPERTY)
PROPERTYGROUP_SHOWGROUPINGOTO_PROPERTY
[@Deprecated](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html)public static final [String](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/String.html) PROPERTYGROUP_SHOWGROUPINGOTO_PROPERTY
Deprecated.
use PropertyGroupStereotype.SHOWGROUPINGOTO
p {padding:0px; margin:0px;}
If the value is *true*, the property group/ subgroup will be visible in element’s shortcut menu as a submenu under the **Go To** menu.
See Also:
[Constant Field Values](../../../constant-values.html#com.nomagic.uml2.MagicDrawProfile.PROPERTYGROUP_SHOWGROUPINGOTO_PROPERTY)
PROPERTYGROUP_SHOWGROUPINQUICKPROPERTIES_PROPERTY
[@Deprecated](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html)public static final [String](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/String.html) PROPERTYGROUP_SHOWGROUPINQUICKPROPERTIES_PROPERTY
Deprecated.
use PropertyGroupStereotype.SHOWGROUPINQUICKPROPERTIES
p {padding:0px; margin:0px;}
If the value is *true*, the property group/ subgroup will be visible in the element’s **Properties** panel.
See Also:
[Constant Field Values](../../../constant-values.html#com.nomagic.uml2.MagicDrawProfile.PROPERTYGROUP_SHOWGROUPINQUICKPROPERTIES_PROPERTY)
PROPERTYGROUP_SHOWGROUPINRELATIONMAP_PROPERTY
[@Deprecated](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html)public static final [String](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/String.html) PROPERTYGROUP_SHOWGROUPINRELATIONMAP_PROPERTY
Deprecated.
use PropertyGroupStereotype.SHOWGROUPINRELATIONMAP
p {padding:0px; margin:0px;}
If the value is *true* , the property group/ subgroup will be visible in the **Criterion Editor** dialog, when you create or edit a Relation Map diagram.
See Also:
[Constant Field Values](../../../constant-values.html#com.nomagic.uml2.MagicDrawProfile.PROPERTYGROUP_SHOWGROUPINRELATIONMAP_PROPERTY)

PROPERTYGROUP_USEASNODE_PROPERTY
[@Deprecated](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html)public static final [String](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/String.html) PROPERTYGROUP_USEASNODE_PROPERTY
Deprecated.
use PropertyGroupStereotype.USEASNODE
p {padding:0px; margin:0px;}
If the value is *true*, the property group will be displayed in a separate tab in the element’s Specification window and/ or **Properties** panel.
 
If the value is *false*, the property group will be displayed as a group in the general (default) pane of the element’s Specification window and/ or **Properties** panel.
 
**Note:** Do not set this property value to *true* for the property subgroup.
See Also:
[Constant Field Values](../../../constant-values.html#com.nomagic.uml2.MagicDrawProfile.PROPERTYGROUP_USEASNODE_PROPERTY)

use getInstance(element).relationMap().getStereotype()
p {padding:0px; margin:0px;}
The **Relation Map** allows rapid creation, review, and analysis of relationships among the elements of the whole model.

**To create a relation map:**
Drag an element from the Model Browser to the relation map.
Specify filters: Relation Criterion, Element Type, Scope, and Depth.

Relation map can display the model structure in two different layouts - **tree** or **radial**.

The relations represented on the map can be analyzed in one of the following ways:
Dynamically - according to the applied filters: element type, dependencies criterion, depth, and scope.
Statically - step-by-step by expanding the branches of the structure.

To represent the same element as a single node in the map, click the Options button on the toolbar and then select the **Show Single Node Per Element** command.


**Manipulations:**

**Create a relation map structure** - drag an element from the Model Browser to the relation map.
**Restore manually suppressed / expanded branches and hidden elements and they position** - click the Refresh button
 .
**Expand / suppress branches** - click
 /
 near the node.
**Move the whole structure** - click an empty space on the relation map and drag.
**Move the selected node** - click the node and drag.
**Insert New Element** - select an element and click
 to choose an element and relationship. Press Insert (Cmd+I for MAC users) to create an element faster and more easily.
**Insert New Element of the Same Type** - type the name and press Ctrl+Enter to create an element of the same type.
**Zoom in** - hold down Ctrl while scrolling up.
**Zoom out** - hold down Ctrl while scrolling down.
**Fit in window** - press Ctrl+W.
**Zoom 1:1** - press Ctrl+NumPad /.
**Turn on the ability to change the context element with single click** - click the Options button on the toolbar and then select the **Make Element as Context on Selection** command.


**Filters:**

**Context** - the core element of the structure.
**Relation Criterion** - relations that are shown. Property, custom Tag definition, or relationship from diagrams can be chosen as a relation.
**Element Type** - element types that are shown.
**Scope** - fragment of the model (or the whole model) from which the relation map is built.
**Depth** - level of branches automatically expanded starting from the context element.
Returns:
stereotype
isRelationMap

A control is a class whose objects manage interactions between collections of objects.
 A control class usually has behavior that is specific for one use case, and a control object usually does not outlive the use case realizations in which it participates.

[@Deprecated](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html)public [Stereotype](ext/magicdraw/mdprofiles/Stereotype.html) getDerivedPropertySpecification()
Deprecated.
use getInstance(element).derivedPropertySpecification().getStereotype()
Stereotype to define derived property. Property owned by DSL customization class should be stereotyped with derivedPropertySpecification in order to be included in MagicDraw as derived property. derivedPropertySpecification element has one or many expression to calculate derived property in different languages. Derived properties result is union of results of defined expressions.
Returns:
stereotype

<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use NumberingSchemeStereotype.NUMBERINGSTYLE</div>
</div>
<div class="block">Select a multi-lever style to number elements in more than one numbering level. Specify at least one number part for each numbering level.
 Select a consecutive style to number elements in one numbering level. Specify only one number part.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>See Also:</dt>
<dd>
<ul class="tag-list">
<li><a href="../../../constant-values.html#com.nomagic.uml2.MagicDrawProfile.NUMBERINGSCHEME_NUMBERINGSTYLE_PROPERTY">Constant Field Values</a></li>

</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>

<section class="detail" id="SMARTPACKAGE_USEINSELECTIONDIALOGS_PROPERTY">
<h3>SMARTPACKAGE_USEINSELECTIONDIALOGS_PROPERTY</h3>
<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>
</span><span class="modifiers">public static final</span> <span class="return-type"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/String.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">String</a></span> <span class="element-name">SMARTPACKAGE_USEINSELECTIONDIALOGS_PROPERTY</span></div>
<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use SmartPackageStereotype.USEINSELECTIONDIALOGS</div>
</div>
<div class="block">This option determines whether contents of this smart package should be calculated and taken into account when operating in various Select Element dialogs. Having this option on may help you to pick elements in Select Element dialog, but it also incurs performance penalty. If your smart package contains a slow-running computation - such as Find - you may want to keep this option off for performance reasons.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>See Also:</dt>
<dd>
<ul class="tag-list">
<li><a href="../../../constant-values.html#com.nomagic.uml2.MagicDrawProfile.SMARTPACKAGE_USEINSELECTIONDIALOGS_PROPERTY">Constant Field Values</a></li>
</ul>
</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>
<li>

<section class="detail" id="DERIVEDPROPERTYSPECIFICATION_EXPRESSION_PROPERTY">
<h3>DERIVEDPROPERTYSPECIFICATION_EXPRESSION_PROPERTY</h3>
<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>
</span><span class="modifiers">public static final</span> <span class="return-type"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/String.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">String</a></span> <span class="element-name">DERIVEDPROPERTYSPECIFICATION_EXPRESSION_PROPERTY</span></div>
<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use DerivedPropertySpecificationStereotype.EXPRESSION</div>
</div>
<div class="block">List of different types of criteria how to gather dependent elements: Simple (UML Relation, Property or Tag); Meta Chain, OCL and Binary. If several criterion are defined results is union of all of them.
 <p>
 Expression can be used in Derived Property Specification, Dependency Matrix, Dependency Map.</p></div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>See Also:</dt>
<dd>
<ul class="tag-list">
<li><a href="../../../constant-values.html#com.nomagic.uml2.MagicDrawProfile.DERIVEDPROPERTYSPECIFICATION_EXPRESSION_PROPERTY">Constant Field Values</a></li>
</ul>
</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>
<li>

<h3>DERIVEDPROPERTYSPECIFICATION_ISREADONLY_PROPERTY</h3>
<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>

<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use DerivedPropertySpecificationStereotype.ISREADONLY</div>
</div>
<div class="block">If True, property is read only without ability to create target element. If False Property is read / write with ability to create value according to defined expressions.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>See Also:</dt>
<dd>
<ul class="tag-list">
<li><a href="../../../constant-values.html#com.nomagic.uml2.MagicDrawProfile.DERIVEDPROPERTYSPECIFICATION_ISREADONLY_PROPERTY">Constant Field Values</a></li>
</ul>
</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>

<section class="detail" id="PROPERTYGROUP_COLUMNS_PROPERTY">
<h3>PROPERTYGROUP_COLUMNS_PROPERTY</h3>
<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>
</span><span class="modifiers">public static final</span> <span class="return-type"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/String.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">String</a></span> <span class="element-name">PROPERTYGROUP_COLUMNS_PROPERTY</span></div>
<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use PropertyGroupStereotype.COLUMNS</div>
</div>
<div class="block"><html>
<head>
<style>
 p {padding:0px; margin:0px;}
 </style>
</head>
<body>
 Stores the specified table columns for showing the values of a multivalued property in the element’s Specification window, for example, <b>ownedOperation</b> in Class.
 <br/><b>Note:</b> Do not specify this property, when there is more than one property assigned to the properties group and/ or subgroup, i.e., when there is more than one value added to the <b>Properties</b> property.
 <br/><b>Note:</b> The property group, to which the multivalued property is assigned, must be specified as a separate tab in the element’s Specification window, for example, the <b>Use As Node</b> property must be set to <i>true</i>.</body>
</html></div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>See Also:</dt>
<dd>
<ul class="tag-list">
<li><a href="../../../constant-values.html#com.nomagic.uml2.MagicDrawProfile.PROPERTYGROUP_COLUMNS_PROPERTY">Constant Field Values</a></li>
</ul>
</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>
<li>
<section class="detail" id="PROPERTYGROUP_FILTER_PROPERTY">

</div>
<div class="block"><html>
<head>
<style>
 p {padding:0px; margin:0px;}
 </style>
</head>
<body>
 Stores the element types (UML metaclasses and custom stereotypes) that will be displayed as property values.</body>
</html></div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>See Also:</dt>

</ul>
</dd>

</section>
</li>

</span><span class="modifiers">public static final</span> <span class="return-type"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/String.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">String</a></span> <span class="element-name">PROPERTYGROUP_PROPERTIES_PROPERTY</span></div>

<div class="deprecation-comment">use PropertyGroupStereotype.PROPERTIES</div>
</div>
<div class="block"><html>
<head>
<style>
 p {padding:0px; margin:0px;}
 </style>
</head>
<body>
 Stores a list of properties that will be visible in the property group or subgroup.
 <br/><b>Note:</b> Do not set this property value to <i>true</i> for the property subgroup.</body>
</html></div>

<dt>See Also:</dt>

<section class="detail" id="PROPERTYGROUP_SHOWGROUPINCOMPARTMENTEDIT_PROPERTY">
<h3>PROPERTYGROUP_SHOWGROUPINCOMPARTMENTEDIT_PROPERTY</h3>
<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>
</span><span class="modifiers">public static final</span> <span class="return-type"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/String.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">String</a></span> <span class="element-name">PROPERTYGROUP_SHOWGROUPINCOMPARTMENTEDIT_PROPERTY</span></div>
<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use PropertyGroupStereotype.SHOWGROUPINCOMPARTMENTEDIT</div>
</div>
<div class="block"><html>
<head>
<style>
 p {padding:0px; margin:0px;}
 </style>
</head>
<body>
 This property allows for displaying the element properties in the <b>Compartment Edit</b> dialog &gt; the <b>Element Properties</b> tab and in a note on a diagram either within the property group/subgroup or without it. If the value is <i>true</i>, the property name will be displayed as follows: <i>&lt;property_group_name&gt;::&lt;property_name&gt;</i>. If the value is <i>false</i>, the property name will be displayed as follows: <i>&lt;property_name&gt;</i>.</body>
</html></div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>See Also:</dt>
<dd>
<ul class="tag-list">
<li><a href="../../../constant-values.html#com.nomagic.uml2.MagicDrawProfile.PROPERTYGROUP_SHOWGROUPINCOMPARTMENTEDIT_PROPERTY">Constant Field Values</a></li>
</ul>
</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>
<li>

 </style>

<section class="detail" id="PROPERTYGROUP_USEASNODE_PROPERTY">
<h3>PROPERTYGROUP_USEASNODE_PROPERTY</h3>
<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>
</span><span class="modifiers">public static final</span> <span class="return-type"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/String.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">String</a></span> <span class="element-name">PROPERTYGROUP_USEASNODE_PROPERTY</span></div>
<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use PropertyGroupStereotype.USEASNODE</div>
</div>
<div class="block"><html>
<head>
<style>
 p {padding:0px; margin:0px;}
 </style>
</head>
<body>
 If the value is <i>true</i>, the property group will be displayed in a separate tab in the element’s Specification window and/ or <b>Properties</b> panel.
 <br/>If the value is <i>false</i>, the property group will be displayed as a group in the general (default) pane of the element’s Specification window and/ or <b>Properties</b> panel.
 <br/><b>Note:</b> Do not set this property value to <i>true</i> for the property subgroup.</body>
</html></div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>See Also:</dt>
<dd>
<ul class="tag-list">
<li><a href="../../../constant-values.html#com.nomagic.uml2.MagicDrawProfile.PROPERTYGROUP_USEASNODE_PROPERTY">Constant Field Values</a></li>
</ul>
</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>
<li>

<li>
<section class="detail" id="getRelationMap()">
<h3>getRelationMap</h3>
<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>
</span><span class="modifiers">public</span> <span class="return-type"><a href="ext/magicdraw/mdprofiles/Stereotype.html" title="interface in com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdprofiles">Stereotype</a></span> <span class="element-name">getRelationMap</span>()</div>
<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).relationMap().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block"><html>
<head>
<style>
 p {padding:0px; margin:0px;}
 </style>
</head>
<body>
<p>
 The <b>Relation Map</b> allows rapid creation, review, and analysis of relationships among the elements of the whole model.

 </p>
<p>
  

 </p>
<p>
<b>To create a relation map: </b>
</p>
<ol>
<li>
 Drag an element from the Model Browser to the relation map.

 </li>
<li>
 Specify filters: Relation Criterion, Element Type, Scope, and Depth.

 </li>
</ol>
<p>
  

 </p>
<p>
 Relation map can display the model structure in two different layouts - <b>tree</b> or <b>radial</b>.

 </p>
<p>
  

 </p>
<p>
 The relations represented on the map can be analyzed in one of the following ways:

 </p>
<p>
 Dynamically - according to the applied filters: element type, dependencies criterion, depth, and scope.

 </p>
<p>
 Statically - step-by-step by expanding the branches of the structure.

 </p>
<p>
  

 </p>
<p>
 To represent the same element as a single node in the map, click the Options button on the toolbar and then select the <b>Show Single Node Per Element</b> command.

 </p>
<p>
  

 </p>
<p>
  

 </p>
<b>Manipulations:</b>
<p>
  

 </p>
<p>
<b>Create a relation map structure</b> - drag an element from the Model Browser to the relation map.

 </p>
<p>
<b>Restore manually suppressed / expanded branches and hidden elements and they position</b> - click the Refresh button
 <img border="0" src="mdp://resource/com/nomagic/magicdraw/icons/refresh.png"/>.

 </p>
<p>
<b>Expand / suppress branches</b> - click
 <img border="0" src="mdp://resource/com/nomagic/magicdraw/visualization/relationshipmap/icons/expand.png"/> /
 <img border="0" src="mdp://resource/com/nomagic/magicdraw/visualization/relationshipmap/icons/supp.png"/> near the node.

 </p>
<p>
<b>Move the whole structure</b> - click an empty space on the relation map and drag.

 </p>
<p>
<b>Move the selected node</b> - click the node and drag.

 </p>
<p>
<b>Insert New Element</b> - select an element and click
 <img border="0" src="mdp://resource/com/nomagic/magicdraw/visualization/relationshipmap/icons/create_node.png"/> to choose an element and relationship. Press Insert (Cmd+I for MAC users) to create an element faster and more easily.

 </p>
<p>
<b>Insert New Element of the Same Type</b> - type the name and press Ctrl+Enter to create an element of the same type.

 </p>
<p>
<b>Zoom in</b> - hold down Ctrl while scrolling up.

 </p>
<p>
<b>Zoom out</b> - hold down Ctrl while scrolling down.

 </p>
<p>
<b>Fit in window</b> - press Ctrl+W.

 </p>
<p>
<b>Zoom 1:1</b> - press Ctrl+NumPad /.

 </p>
<p>
<b>Turn on the ability to change the context element with single click</b> - click the Options button on the toolbar and then select the <b>Make Element as Context on Selection</b> command.

 </p>
<p>
  

 </p>
<p>
  

 </p>
<p>
<b>Filters:</b>
</p>
<p>
  

 </p>
<p>
<b>Context</b> - the core element of the structure.

 </p>
<p>
<b>Relation Criterion</b> - relations that are shown. Property, custom Tag definition, or relationship from diagrams can be chosen as a relation.

 </p>
<p>
<b>Element Type</b> - element types that are shown.

 </p>
<p>
<b>Scope</b> - fragment of the model (or the whole model) from which the relation map is built.

 </p>
<p>
<b>Depth</b> - level of branches automatically expanded starting from the context element.

 </p>
</body>
</html></div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>
<li>

</span><span class="modifiers">public static</span> <span class="return-type">boolean</span> <span class="element-name">isRelationMap</span><wbr/><span class="parameters">(@CheckForNull
 <a href="ext/magicdraw/classes/mdkernel/Element.html" title="interface in com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel">Element</a> element)</span></div>

</div>
<div class="block">A boundary is a class that lies on the periphery of a system, but within it. It interacts with actors outside the system as well as with entity, control, and other boundary classes within the system.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>

<section class="detail" id="getControl()">

<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>

<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).control().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">A control is a class whose objects manage interactions between collections of objects.
 A control class usually has behavior that is specific for one use case, and a control object usually does not outlive the use case realizations in which it participates.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>

<section class="detail" id="getDerivedPropertySpecification()">
<h3>getDerivedPropertySpecification</h3>
<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/21/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>
</span><span class="modifiers">public</span> <span class="return-type"><a href="ext/magicdraw/mdprofiles/Stereotype.html" title="interface in com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdprofiles">Stereotype</a></span> <span class="element-name">getDerivedPropertySpecification</span>()</div>
<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).derivedPropertySpecification().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">Stereotype to define derived property. Property owned by DSL customization class should be stereotyped with derivedPropertySpecification in order to be included in MagicDraw as derived property. derivedPropertySpecification element has one or many expression to calculate derived property in different languages. Derived properties result is union of results of defined expressions.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>
